title: Kioxia and Sandisk sample world's densest 3D NAND new 332-Layer beats Samsung s 400-Layer NAND
summary: Kioxia and SanDisk have begun sampling their BiCS10 3D NAND technology, which boasts 332 active layers and an areal capacity of over 29 Gb/mm2.
insight: Industry leaders push storage density limits with record-breaking innovation.

title: Unix copyright code infringement lawsuit is back from the dead IBM still under fire from Xinuos over 2003-era bytes
summary: IBM faces lawsuit from Xinuos over alleged Unix copyright infringement from 2003-era code. A recent hearing was held on June 22. The case appears to be ongoing.
insight: Legacy code copyright disputes can still lead to prolonged litigation

title: Modder builds 8,192-core GPU at home out of RISC-V microcontrollers full "graphics card" draws over 2,000 watts of power, requires a 3D printer to program
summary: Matthias Balwierz, aka Bitluni, created a massive 8,192-core RISC-V GPU at home. This custom GPU draws over 2,000 watts of power and requires a 3D printer for programming. The project showcases the potential of DIY computer engineering.
insight: RISC-V architecture enables high-performance computing at unprecedented scales.

title: Intel patent reveals new XBM memory architecture that ditches HBM's costly silicon interposer backend-transistor DRAM stack uses UCIe links and built-in repair to ease AI's memory bottleneck
summary: Intel's XBM patent offers a cost-effective alternative to HBM memory, utilizing backend-transistor DRAM and UCIe links.
insight: Revolutionizing memory architecture for faster, cheaper AI data processing.

title: Samsung chip division's single-year profits beat its past 40 years of profits, combined, due to increased memory and storage prices Samsung passes Nvidia to become most profitable company in the world, notches 19x quarterly increase in profit
summary: Samsung's chip division sees a record-breaking year with 19x quarterly profit increase, surpassing Nvidia to become the most profitable company in the world. The significant jump in memory and storage prices contributes to the substantial profit growth. The brokerage consensus estimates Samsung's full-year 2026 operating profit near 300 trillion won.
insight: Supply chain dominance drives historic profits in chip market.

title: China-made CXMT memory now supports faster speeds on MSI's AMD motherboards new BIOS adds DDR5-8200 validation on dual-DIMM, DDR5-7200 on quad-DIMM models
summary: MSI has validated CXMT RAM for AMD motherboards, achieving speeds up to 8,200 MT/s. The dual-DIMM models can handle these high frequencies better than quad-DIMM variants. This expansion supports region-bound Chinese RAM for faster speeds.
insight: Chinese memory modules achieve high speeds with MSI's AM5 motherboard validation.

title: Microsoft 'resets' Xbox by cutting 3,200 jobs this year, divesting five game studios firm cites 'margins that are 3-10x lower than comparable platform and publishing businesses'
summary: Microsoft's Xbox division to cut 3,200 jobs and divest five studios, citing low margins.
insight: Xbox's cost structure is uncompetitive with other gaming businesses.

title: Working prototype of open-source printer that promises user-repairability and no subscriptions appears in first video DRM-free 'Open Printer' inkjet still has no announced price, ship date, or print speed, nine months after it first appeared
summary: Open Tools' Open Printer, an open-source inkjet printer, has been nominated for two French Design Awards. The printer promises user-repairability and no subscription fees. Its release details remain unknown.
insight: Innovative open-source design challenges traditional printer business models.

title: AMD Ryzen AI Halo review: AMD builds a DGX Spark of its own
summary: The Ryzen AI Halo offers turn-key AI capabilities with first-party software support, but its performance and application compatibility trail Nvidia's offerings and it's priced high.
insight: AI hardware competition intensifies with AMD's Ryzen AI Halo launch.

title: You can now use your Sony headphones as a free real-time head tracker for race and flight simulators on PC, several hundred games already supported enthusiast creates open-source app that translates live sensor data into in-game camera controls
summary: A new open-source app called Sony Head Tracker translates raw sensor data from Sony headphones and earbuds into head tracking controls for over 200 PC games, enhancing gameplay experiences.
insight: Real-time head tracking enhances immersion in PC games and simulators.

title: Nvidia's Kyber rack for Rubin Ultra reportedly delayed to 2028, stopgap solution also axed due to customer pushback Analyst firm SemiAnalysis says PCB midplane problems led to the delay [Updated]
summary: Nvidia's Kyber rack for Rubin Ultra is reportedly delayed to 2028. The delay is attributed to PCB midplane problems. This is a setback for customers relying on this product.
insight: Nvidia's high-performance product delay impacts customers' data infrastructure plans

title: Nvidia and Intel tout homegrown American chip supply chain prowess as country bolsters local production, but gaps remain crucial Blackwell packaging steps remain offshore as projects grow in scope and scale
summary: Nvidia and Intel's homegrown American chip supply chains have limitations.
insight: Gaps in Blackwell packaging hinder American AI chip production dominance.

title: Electric drone breaks world air speed record at 434 mph, designed for anti-aircraft interceptor roles German firm convincingly smashed the official 409 mph record, hopes to get stamp of approval from Guinness soon
summary: A German firm's electric drone, Apex Recordhunter, broke the world air speed record with a top speed of 434 mph during internal testing, surpassing the previous 409 mph record.
insight: Electric drones may revolutionize anti-aircraft interceptor technology and applications.

title: China s Huawei to enter South Korean AI chip market with new Atlas SuperPods, clusters pack 8,192 Ascend 950 accelerators per deployment reportedly challenges Nvidia dominance with 'tripled inference performance' of H20 at one-quarter the cost
summary: Huawei enters South Korea's AI chip market with Atlas SuperPods, challenging Nvidia's dominance.
insight: Aggressive pricing and improved performance challenge Nvidia's market control

title: ESP32-C5 devkit offers 2.8-inch capacitive touch display, dual-band Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5 LE, LiPo battery support
summary: The ESP32-C5-Touch-LCD-2.8 devkit features a 2.8-inch capacitive touch display, dual-band Wi-Fi 6, and Bluetooth 5 LE connectivity. It also includes a microSD card, USB-C port, microphone, and speaker connector. This board is suitable for IoT and HMI projects.
insight: Enhanced user interaction with capacitive touch display features

title: Mercedes-Benz hosts open-source hardware Automotive Rapid DEvelopment Platform (ARDEP)
summary: Mercedes-Benz has released an open-source hardware platform called ARDEP, hosted on their GitHub account, featuring an STMicroelectronics STM32G474VE microcontroller with various transceivers and accelerators.
insight: Accelerated automotive development with open-source hardware and software collaboration.

title: PocketMage An ESP32-S3-based Personal Digital Assistant (Crowdfunding)
summary: PocketMage is a personal digital assistant with a 3.1-inch E Ink display and a full tactile QWERTY keyboard. It features Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and a microSD card slot for expansion. The device is built around an ESP32-S3 microcontroller.
insight: Reviving the PDA concept with modern wireless connectivity and compact design.

title: SMP Techstrong joint venture expands sensor manufacturing
summary: SMP, a New York-based company, has formed a 50/50 joint venture with Techstrong Holdings, a Thailand-based company, to expand their vehicle sensor capabilities.
insight: Enhancing vehicle safety through advanced sensor technology development

title: Nvidia RTX 5080 Super with 415W TDP appears in Seasonic PSU calculator
summary: The Nvidia RTX 5080 Super's 415W TDP is confirmed, aligning with previous 400+W rumors. This information is based on the Seasonic PSU wattage calculator listing. The TDP is a crucial factor in choosing a suitable power supply.
insight: Power supply requirements for RTX 5080 Super become more predictable

title: AMD X870 Motherboard Roundup: 53 Motherboards Tested
summary: A comprehensive review of 53 AMD X870 and X870E motherboards, testing VRM thermals, USB performance, and features.
insight: Motherboard quality and features greatly impact overall system performance and reliability.

title: Microsoft is cutting 3,200 Xbox jobs and spinning off four game studios
summary: Microsoft is cutting 3,200 Xbox jobs and spinning off four game studios. The immediate layoffs will impact 1,600 employees. Additional redundancies will occur across other Microsoft divisions.
insight: Massive restructuring threatens Xbox's market position and developer morale

title: Steam Machine owner reports a "Red Line of Death" days after launch, but it fixed itself
summary: A Steam Machine owner reported a 'Red Line of Death' issue, but it resolved on its own. This incident raises concerns about the device's reliability. The Steam Machine's launch is still in its early stages.
insight: Early reliability issues may impact Steam Machine's long-term consumer trust.

title: Microsoft has a hidden Windows 11 edition that supports 6TB of RAM and 256 CPU cores
summary: Windows 11 Pro for Workstations supports up to 6TB of RAM and 256 CPU cores, making it suitable for professionals and enterprise users with demanding workloads.
insight: Unleashing unparalleled performance for data-intensive and compute-heavy applications

title: Nvidia Profile Inspector 3.0 modernizes the popular driver profile editor
summary: Nvidia Profile Inspector 3.0 features a new interface, faster browsing, and support for newer features.
insight: Enhances driver customization for improved gaming performance and visuals.

title: Why Gen Z is falling in love with film photography and rejecting the algorithm along with it
summary: Gen Z is embracing film photography as a counter to algorithm-driven digital culture, seeking a more authentic and tactile experience. This resurgence is driven by a desire to break free from the curated and superficial nature of social media. By using film cameras, they're able to capture genuine moments and express themselves in a more organic way.
insight: Authenticity and human connection in a digitally curated world.

title: Philips' new "triple-mode" monitors go all the way down to 720p for maximum speed
summary: Philips' Evnia monitors offer triple-mode display, switching between gaming modes quickly.
insight: Adaptive display modes for optimal gaming performance and efficiency.

title: Samsung will earn more profit in 2026 than it did over the previous 40 years combined thanks to the AI boom
summary: Samsung's 2026 profit is expected to exceed its cumulative profit from 1980-2025, driven by the AI boom.
insight: AI-driven growth to shatter Samsung's 40-year profit record.

title: SpaceX intentionally burned up 260 Starlink satellites in the last six months, with hundreds more set to follow
summary: SpaceX intentionally burns up Starlink satellites at end of 5-year lifespan, reentering atmosphere.
insight: Reducing space debris through controlled satellite reentry is crucial conservation.

title: One of Windows' most hated features just helped catch a 19-year-old hacker
summary: Finnish police arrested 19-year-old Peter Stokes, a dual US-Estonian citizen, for hacking charges. He was extradited to the US, where he faces six counts of conspiracy, computer intrusion, and fraud. The arrest was made possible by a Windows feature.
insight: Windows' digital footprints can be used to catch cybercriminals worldwide effectively.

title: The Supreme Court just ruled that police need a warrant to use phone location data
summary: The Supreme Court ruled that police need a warrant to use phone location data for geofence warrants, which involves collecting data from devices in a specific area.
insight: This impacts civil liberties by limiting mass surveillance capabilities.

title: Nvidia's RTX 3060 returns to store shelves at its original 2021 price
summary: The Nvidia RTX 3060 is back in stock at Newegg for $329.99, its original 2021 launch price. The listing was created on July 2 and published on July 5. This marks a significant price drop for the graphics card.
insight: Nvidia RTX 3060 returns to affordable prices after long price surge.
